title: Vercel vs Netlify
description: Two leading frontend deployment platforms compared head-to-head.
verdict: Vercel provides deep Next.js optimization with Incremental Static Regeneration and robust edge functions that enable highly dynamic sites at the edge. Netlify pioneered the Jamstack ecosystem with excellent built-in features like Netlify Forms, CMS support, and split testing, all on a generous free tier. Teams building with Next.js and React frameworks will get the best performance and developer experience from Vercel's tightly integrated platform. Teams running simpler static sites, Jamstack projects, or sites that need form handling and CMS capabilities will find Netlify's mature feature set more immediately useful.

faqs.question: Which is better, Vercel or Netlify?
faqs.answer: Vercel is generally better for Next.js projects and teams needing advanced edge rendering with ISR. Netlify is a great choice for simpler static sites, Jamstack projects, and teams that want built-in form handling and CMS support.

faqs.question: Is Vercel free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vercel offers a generous free tier with 100 GB bandwidth, 6000 build minutes per month, and unlimited serverless function invocations. Paid plans start for teams needing more resources.

faqs.question: Can I host a static site on Vercel?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vercel supports any static site including Hugo, Jekyll, and plain HTML/CSS/JS sites, not just Next.js projects. Netlify has similar support for static site generators.

faqs.question: Which platform has better performance?
faqs.answer: Vercel generally offers faster edge responses due to its global network and ISR capabilities. Netlify's performance is also excellent, but Vercel has an edge for dynamic content served from the edge.
